notes.progression: All teams are defined at max level (level 100). Each time a gym leader loses a challenge their Pokemon gain 2–4 levels each, weighted toward the team average so stragglers catch up. Max level is 100. Normal NPCs follow the same loss-driven progression but with a lower ceiling — max 6 Pokemon at tier 6 (level 65 threshold), no megas, no legendaries.

levelMilestones.66-80: 6 Pokemon, full items, mega stone active
levelMilestones.81+: 6 Pokemon, full items, mega stone active, legendary slot unlocked

notes.fightEstimates: At avg 3 levels per loss: mega unlocks around fight 26–28, legendary around fight 32–35.
notes.startingThree: The first three slots form the starting team at level 1. As the leader levels into higher bands, subsequent slots are added in order.
notes.poolReset: Once a theme is selected by any player on the server it is removed from the available pool. When 3 themes remain the pool resets to all 25.
notes.megaEvolution: Each leader has one Pokemon holding a mega stone. Its position in the team reflects its role — some are leads, some are mid-game pivots, some are closers. The mega stone is only present at level 66+.
notes.legendary: Each leader has one legendary assigned that replaces a specific team slot at level 81+. The replaced Pokemon is noted per leader. Two leaders (Revenant, Plague Doctor) have no mega — their legendary is their only late-game escalation.

gymLeaders.name: The Wanderer
gymLeaders.description: A trainer who has journeyed every region. One companion from each corner of the world.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Salamence: Aerilate turns Return into a 130BP Flying STAB — the sky wanderer who has crossed every region

legendary.note: The roaming thunder beast — arrives when the wanderer's journey is complete

gymLeaders.name: The Ancient
gymLeaders.description: A fossil researcher who has brought titans of the prehistoric world back to life.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Aerodactyl: Tough Claws boosts all contact moves — the king of the ancient skies

legendary.note: The original stone titan — ancient before even the fossils on this team

gymLeaders.name: The Berserker
gymLeaders.description: No strategy, no patience. Every Pokemon carries a Choice item and swings for maximum damage from turn one.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Heracross: Skill Link makes all multi-hit moves land 5× — Pin Missile becomes 75BP Bug STAB, Rock Blast 125BP coverage

legendary.note: Pure aggression, no strategy — the Berserker's ultimate weapon

gymLeaders.name: The Fortress
gymLeaders.description: An impenetrable wall. Every Pokemon stalls, heals, and outlasts. You will not break through.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Aggron: Filter reduces super-effective damage; pure Steel type — an immovable fortress

legendary.note: The ultimate defensive wall — Moonlight recovery makes it nearly unkillable

gymLeaders.name: The Zephyr
gymLeaders.description: Before you can act, it is already over. The fastest team alive, built around priority and first-turn pressure.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Aerodactyl: Tough Claws — the closer; by the time it lands it's already moving too fast to stop
Mega Blaziken: Speed Boost — Protect on turn 1 banks a free boost, then it mega evolves and nothing outruns it

legendary.note: The fastest Electric legendary — replaces Electrode at the speed limit

gymLeaders.name: The Storm
gymLeaders.description: Rain gives way to sandstorm, sun follows behind. A team of weather setters and abusers that keeps the battlefield in perpetual chaos.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Tyranitar: Sand Stream persists; higher Attack and Defense — the weather war's ultimate weapon

legendary.note: The origin of rain — Castform was always the gimmick, this is the real weather war

gymLeaders.name: The Celestial
gymLeaders.description: A stargazer whose team fell from the sky. Pokemon of the cosmos, moons, and deep space.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Ampharos: Mold Breaker, Dragon/Electric — the wool dragon of deep space

legendary.note: The actual moon — Lunatone was always a placeholder

gymLeaders.name: The Noblesse
gymLeaders.description: Regal, elegant, and devastating. Beauty is not weakness — it is the most dangerous weapon of all.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Gardevoir: Pixilate converts Hyper Voice to 130BP Fairy — the crown jewel of the team

legendary.note: Geomancy into Fairy Aura Moonblast — the crown jewel of elegance

gymLeaders.name: The Corsair
gymLeaders.description: A sea-faring outlaw with a crew of ocean predators. Fast, aggressive, and utterly relentless.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Sharpedo: Strong Jaw; Protect on turn 1 to bank a Speed Boost, then mega and sweep

legendary.note: Guardian of the sea — the corsair's ultimate enforcer

gymLeaders.name: The Inventor
gymLeaders.description: Artificial lifeforms, mechanical constructs, and digital ghosts. Nothing on this team was born — it was built.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Metagross: Tough Claws; the most advanced construct on the team

legendary.note: A fossil weaponised by science — the ultimate artificial construct

gymLeaders.name: The Underdog
gymLeaders.description: Every Pokemon on this team is underestimated. Eviolite bulks up the unevolved. Do not assume this will be easy.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Beedrill: Adaptability — the ultimate underdog story; was unviable, now terrifying

legendary.note: The underdog of the Swords of Justice — had to earn its legendary status

gymLeaders.name: The Cursed
gymLeaders.description: A team built around one promise: even if you win, something bad is going to happen to you.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Gengar: Shadow Tag — you cannot escape. Pairs with Destiny Bond for guaranteed trades

legendary.note: The nightmare — everything it touches falls into bad dreams

gymLeaders.name: The Nightshade
gymLeaders.description: Night hunters. They do not announce themselves. By the time you see them coming, it is too late.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Houndoom: Solar Power; the hellhound that emerges from the dark mid-battle

legendary.note: The shadow that was there the whole time

gymLeaders.name: The Veteran
gymLeaders.description: A trainer who has been playing since the very beginning. The original legends — optimised for the modern game.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Alakazam: Trace copies the opponent's ability; Sp.Atk 175 — the Gen 1 ace at its peak

legendary.note: The original Gen 1 endgame — the veteran's true ace

gymLeaders.name: The Empress
gymLeaders.description: Every Pokemon arrives with one goal: find a moment to set up, then sweep the entire team. Snowball or die trying.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Lucario: Adaptability — Aura Sphere becomes 240 effective BP after one Nasty Plot

legendary.note: The Victory Pokemon — closes out every sweep

gymLeaders.name: The Revenant
gymLeaders.description: They do not stay down. Sturdy, Regenerator, Focus Sash — every Pokemon on this team finds a way to come back.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Gyarados: Mold Breaker, Water/Dark — rises from defeat again and again

legendary.note: Banished but always returns — the ultimate revenant

gymLeaders.name: The Pixie
gymLeaders.description: Small. Cute. Absolutely devastating. Do not be fooled by the aesthetics.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Lopunny: Scrappy hits Ghost types; one of the fastest Normal/Fighting threats in the game

legendary.note: Soul-Heart boosts Sp.Atk every time something faints — small, cute, devastating

gymLeaders.name: The Sovereign
gymLeaders.description: Six pseudo-legendaries. No gimmicks. No tricks. Just the six most powerful non-legendary Pokemon in existence.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Garchomp: Sand Force; the pinnacle of pseudo-legendaries — raw power with no gimmick needed

legendary.note: The Hero of Many Battles — raw power, no gimmicks, the only worthy replacement for a pseudo-legendary roster

gymLeaders.name: The Oracle
gymLeaders.description: A seer who fights two turns ahead. Future Sight and Doom Desire land while you're focused on the present.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Alakazam: Trace copies the opponent's ability — the seer who already knows what you're going to do

legendary.note: Sees through rings across time and space — the oracle's true form revealed

gymLeaders.name: The Mercenary
gymLeaders.description: Never commits. Always pivoting. A team built entirely around momentum — U-turn, Volt Switch, Flip Turn. You never fight what you expect.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Scizor: Technician + Bullet Punch priority — the pivoting anchor that always finds an angle

legendary.note: The definitive competitive pivot — Intimidate on switch, U-turn out, Earthquake for anything that stays

gymLeaders.name: The Devoted
gymLeaders.description: Every Pokemon on this team evolved through friendship and trust. They fight not for dominance — but for the bond.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Gardevoir: Pixilate converts Hyper Voice to 130BP Fairy — evolved through trust, now fights for the bond

legendary.note: The original companion — bonds with every trainer it meets

gymLeaders.name: The Mythmaker
gymLeaders.description: Pokemon that exist in legend and folklore — not quite legendary, but the stuff of myth. The kitsune, the sea serpent, the omen-bringer.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Absol: Magic Bounce — the omen-bearer that reflects curses back at those who cast them

legendary.note: The rainbow phoenix of legend — the mythmaker's ultimate omen

gymLeaders.name: The Ghost Town
gymLeaders.description: Keepers of abandoned places — ancient golems, haunted clay, and the spirits of buildings long forgotten.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Banette: Prankster gives priority to Will-O-Wisp and Destiny Bond — the haunted doll that drags you down with it

legendary.note: The spectral horse haunting the abandoned town — Grim Neigh boosts Sp.Atk on each KO

gymLeaders.name: The Plague Doctor
gymLeaders.description: Burns, poisons, paralysis. Every Pokemon inflicts a different ailment, then exploits it. Attrition warfare.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Venusaur: Thick Fat; Sleep Powder into Leech Seed — the plague doctor's slow, inevitable drain

legendary.note: Controls others through toxins — Poison Puppeteer confuses anything it poisons

gymLeaders.name: The Warlord
gymLeaders.description: A conquering general and their elite guard. Disciplined, aggressive, built for sustained military campaigns.

maxTeam.megaNote: Mega Gallade: Inner Focus; higher Speed and Attack — the general who leads every charge from the front

legendary.note: General of the Swords of Justice — the warlord's legendary commander
